commands.review_summary:
The branch removes the shared Control UI header `state.lastError` pill and adds jsdom regression tests asserting chat errors stay routed to the Chat view.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +41. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main renders `.page-meta .pill.danger` whenever `state.lastError` is set and also passes that same value to Chat. I did not run a browser session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the branch must preserve non-chat error visibility and the contributor still needs real browser proof; this proof-gated external PR should not be routed to automated repair.

target.title: fix(ui): keep transient chat errors out of page headers
target.body:
## Summary
- Remove the global `lastError` pill from the shared Control UI page header.
- Keep chat errors visible through the existing chat view error path, where `renderChat` receives `state.lastError` and renders the dismissible chat callout.
- Add regression coverage that non-chat pages do not render transient chat errors in `.page-meta`, while the chat page still routes the error into the chat view.

## Context
PR #88248 intentionally made chat failures visible as assistant/chat errors. That behavior is still preserved here.

The regression is that the shared non-config page header also rendered the global `state.lastError`, so a transient chat/runtime error could briefly appear as a red header pill on unrelated pages such as Usage or Sessions.

## Verification
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Transient chat errors no longer leak into non-chat Control UI page headers such as Usage or Sessions.

Real environment tested: Local jsdom UI render test.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: The focused test asserts a Sessions render with `lastError` has no `.page-meta .pill.danger`, and a Chat render with `lastError` has no shared-header error pill while still passing the error to `renderChat`.

Observed result after fix: Chat errors remain visible through the chat view, and unrelated page headers stay clean.

What was not tested: Full browser E2E and full CI matrix.
target.author: sallyom
